info.title: Luma API
info.description: API for interacting with Luma's event platform.

get.description: Return admin information about an event that you have manage access for.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

zoom_meeting_url.description: Use `meeting_url` instead. Not all meetings use Zoom.

get.description:
Every event and API key on Luma is managed by a [Luma Calendar](https://help.luma.com/p/luma-calendar-overview). You can list all of the events managed by your Calendar by using this API route.

Note that this API route will not list events that are listed on the Calendar but not managed by the Calendar.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

zoom_meeting_url.description: Use `meeting_url` instead. Not all meetings use Zoom.

get.description:
Get detailed information for an event guest by looking them up by their ID. This supports looking them up by different parameters.

This response includes `event_ticket_orders`, which includes `coupon_info` when a coupon was applied.

invited_at.description: When the guest was invited to the event. Null if they registered on their own.

joined_at.description: When the guest first clicked the virtual join link (luma.com/join/...). Only set for online events. Null if the guest has not joined.

registered_at.description: When the guest registered for the event.

utm_source.description: The UTM source parameter captured when the guest registered (from the `?utm_source=` query parameter).

checked_in_at.description: Check-in is now per-ticket. Use `checked_in_at` on each item in `event_tickets` instead.

coupon_info.description: The coupon that was applied to this ticket order, if any.

event_ticket_orders.description: Detailed ticket orders for this guest. Use this to inspect applied coupon_info per order.

get.description:
Get list of guests who have registered or been invited to an event.

This list response includes guest summaries and `event_tickets`, but not order-level details like `event_ticket_orders`. Use `/v1/event/get-guest` when you need detailed ticket order data for a specific guest.

invited_at.description: When the guest was invited to the event. Null if they registered on their own.

joined_at.description: When the guest first clicked the virtual join link (luma.com/join/...). Only set for online events. Null if the guest has not joined.

registered_at.description: When the guest registered for the event.

utm_source.description: The UTM source parameter captured when the guest registered (from the `?utm_source=` query parameter).

checked_in_at.description: Check-in is now per-ticket. Use `checked_in_at` on each item in `event_tickets` instead.

get.description: List all admins for the calendar.
Lookup an entity on Luma by it's slug.
See if an event already exists on the calendar. This is useful when figuring out if you want to submit an event to the calendar.

get.description: List all calendars belonging to the organization.

cover_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN. You can upload an image after getting an upload URL from the Luma API.

description_md.description: Luma stores rich text in a special format called Spark. We don't expose an API for that format, but we can convert Markdown to Spark for you. Some Spark features, like Luma event embeds, are not supported by Markdown.

max_capacity.description:
Once an event hits the max capacity, the event will be marked as sold out and registration will automatically be closed.

If `can_register_for_multiple_tickets` is `true`, each ticket will count towards the max capacity.

name_requirement.description: Luma collects the name of every guest as they register. Choose if you'd like to split this into two fields to encourage guests to enter their first and last name.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

reminders_disabled.description: By default, Luma will send reminders before the event. Turn off if you'd like more control over event communications.

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests. If not specified on create, inherits from calendar defaults.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

show_guest_list.description: When the guest list is shown, approved guests will be able to see who else is going to the event.

slug.description:
The URL slug for the event. If the slug is `aloha`, the URL will be `https://luma.com/aloha`. If the slug is not available, the event creation will fail.

You can use `/public/v1/entity/lookup` to check if a slug is available.

tint_color.description: A hex color like '#bb2dc7'. Alpha channels (#rgba, #rrggbbaa) are automatically stripped.

suppress_notifications.description: If true, Luma will not notify guests (via email or push notification) when updating the event name, time, or location.

cover_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN. You can upload an image after getting an upload URL from the Luma API.

description_md.description: Luma stores rich text in a special format called Spark. We don't expose an API for that format, but we can convert Markdown to Spark for you. Some Spark features, like Luma event embeds, are not supported by Markdown.

max_capacity.description:
Once an event hits the max capacity, the event will be marked as sold out and registration will automatically be closed.

If `can_register_for_multiple_tickets` is `true`, each ticket will count towards the max capacity.

name_requirement.description: Luma collects the name of every guest as they register. Choose if you'd like to split this into two fields to encourage guests to enter their first and last name.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

reminders_disabled.description: By default, Luma will send reminders before the event. Turn off if you'd like more control over event communications.

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests. If not specified on create, inherits from calendar defaults.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

show_guest_list.description: When the guest list is shown, approved guests will be able to see who else is going to the event.

slug.description:
The URL slug for the event. If the slug is `aloha`, the URL will be `https://luma.com/aloha`. If the slug is not available, the event creation will fail.

You can use `/public/v1/entity/lookup` to check if a slug is available.

tint_color.description: A hex color like '#bb2dc7'. Alpha channels (#rgba, #rrggbbaa) are automatically stripped.

schema.description: Empty response

should_refund.description: If you are declining a guest and they have paid, should we refund their payment?

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Send guest an invite to an event. We will send an email and if there phone number is linked to their Luma account, they will also receive an SMS.

guests.description: Information about the guests you want to add to the event. If a user already has a name, this will not overwrite their name.

message.description: Personalize the message that will be included in the invite. Max of 200 characters.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add guests to the event. They will be added with the status "Going". By default, guests receive one ticket of the default ticket type. Use the `ticket` or `tickets` parameter to specify custom ticket assignments. Get available ticket types using `/v1/event/ticket-types/list`.

items.description: Information about the guests you want to add to the event. If a user already has a name, this will not overwrite their name.

ticket.description: Optional. Assign a single ticket of the specified type to each guest. Cannot be used with `tickets` parameter.

tickets.description: Optional. Assign multiple tickets to each guest. Each guest receives all tickets in the array. Use this to add multiple tickets of the same or different types. Cannot be used with `ticket` parameter.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add a host to an event. This works for both managers and check-in staff — use the access_level parameter to control permissions.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

name.description: Name of the host you are adding. If they already have a Luma profile, this will be ignored.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Update a host's permissions or visibility on an event. Use this to change a host between check-in and manager access levels. The event creator's access level cannot be changed.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

email.description: Email of the host to update.

access_level.description: The new access level for the host. One of "none", "check-in", or "manager". The event creator's access level cannot be changed.

is_visible.description: Whether the host is visible on the event page.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Remove a host from an event. The event creator cannot be removed.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

email.description: Email of the host to remove.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a coupon that can be applied when a guest is registering for an event. You are not able to edit the coupon terms after it has been created.

code.description: This is the code that the user will enter on the event page. It is not case sensitive. Maximum 20 characters.

remaining_count.description: Number of times the coupon can be used. Set to 1000000 for unlimited uses.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a coupon that can be applied to any event that is managed by the calendar. Be careful not to have the same code on an event and on the calendar.

code.description: This is the code that the user will enter on the event page. It is not case sensitive. Maximum 20 characters.

remaining_count.description: Number of times the coupon can be used. Set to 1000000 for unlimited uses.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Import people to your calendar to easily invite them to events and send them newsletters.

infos.description: Information about the people you want to import. You can include a name and email. Note that we will not set the name of a user once it has already been set.

tag_api_ids.description: The list of tags to apply to people once you have imported them into your calendar. This can be used to import new members with tags and to tag existing members.

tag_names.description: Note: we will not create tags that do not exist. If you want to use a new tag, create a tag before importing people.

schema.description: Empty response
Empty response
Empty response

post.description: Apply a tag to existing calendar members. This will not create new members.

tag.description: Tag API ID (e.g., 'tag-123') or tag name

user_api_ids.description: Array of user API IDs to apply the tag to

emails.description: Array of email addresses to apply the tag to

applied_count.description: Number of members the tag was successfully applied to

skipped_count.description: Number of users skipped because they are not calendar members

post.description: Unapply a tag from calendar members. Only affects existing members.

tag.description: Tag API ID (e.g., 'tag-123') or tag name

user_api_ids.description: Array of user API IDs to remove the tag from

emails.description: Array of email addresses to remove the tag from

removed_count.description: Number of members the tag was successfully removed from

skipped_count.description: Number of users skipped because they are not calendar members

schema.description: Empty response
Empty response

post.description: Apply a tag to events on the calendar.

tag.description: Tag API ID (e.g., 'tag-123') or tag name

event_api_ids.description: Array of event API IDs to apply the tag to

applied_count.description: Number of events the tag was successfully applied to

skipped_count.description: Number of events skipped because they are not on this calendar

post.description: Unapply a tag from events on the calendar.

tag.description: Tag API ID (e.g., 'tag-123') or tag name

event_api_ids.description: Array of event API IDs to remove the tag from

removed_count.description: Number of events the tag was successfully removed from

skipped_count.description: Number of events skipped because they are not on this calendar

post.description:
Add or submit an existing event (on Luma or on an external platform) to the Luma calendar. By default, events added by a calendar manager are approved immediately. Use `submission_mode: "pending"` to keep the submission pending.

On calendars with `require-manage-access`, submitting a Luma event you manage can also grant the target calendar manage access once the submission is approved.

submission_mode.description: `auto` approves the event immediately. `pending` keeps the event pending for calendar admin approval.
`auto` approves the event immediately. `pending` keeps the event pending for calendar admin approval.

upload_url.description: You'll upload your image here using a PUT request.

post.description: Create a new ticket type for an event
Update an existing ticket type configuration
Soft delete a ticket type. Cannot delete if tickets have been sold or if it's the last visible ticket type.

event_ticket_type_api_id.description: Event ticket type API ID to delete

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add a user to a membership tier. For paid tiers, set skip_payment to true if you are handling payment externally.

skip_payment.description: Set to true for paid tiers when handling payment externally. Required for paid tiers.

post.description: Update a member's membership status. Approving a paid tier member captures their payment. Declining cancels any active subscription.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a new webhook endpoint to receive event notifications.
Update a webhook endpoint's event types or status (active/paused).
Delete a webhook endpoint.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Request a cancellation token for an event. Canceling an event is irreversible — it notifies all guests, processes refunds, and permanently deletes the event. Because of this, cancellation uses a two-step flow: first request a token here, then pass it to /event/cancel to execute. The token expires after 15 minutes.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

cancellation_token.description: A short-lived token to confirm the cancellation. Expires after 15 minutes.

is_paid.description: Whether the event has paid guests. If true, you must pass should_refund in the confirm step.

guest_count.description: The number of approved guests for this event.

post.description: Cancel an event. You must first call /event/cancel/request to get a cancellation_token, then pass it here. This is irreversible — the event will be deleted, all guests will be notified, and refunds will be processed if should_refund is true. For paid events, you must specify should_refund.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

cancellation_token.description: The cancellation token returned from the request endpoint.

should_refund.description: Whether to refund paid guests. Required if the event has paid guests.

schema.description: Empty response

apiKeyAuth.name: x-luma-api-key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key to authenticate requests to the Luma API. Generate an API key on your Luma dashboard.

post.description: Triggered when a new event is created on your calendar.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

200.description: Return a 2xx status to acknowledge receipt of the webhook.
